commit.message:
fix: moon element print offset - don't perturb arc radii

Arc radius perturbation in perturbPath() shifted the computed arc center,
causing visible misalignment between concentric arcs (body vs craters).
Now only arc endpoint coordinates are perturbed, preserving circle center
alignment while still providing organic edge variation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persistent "Save as my default" after preference toast dismissal (#154)

* feat: add persistent "Save as my default" affordance for preferences

- Add hasUnsavedChanges and saveCurrentValue to useDeferredBooleanPreference
- Add hasUnsavedChanges and saveCurrentValue to useDeferredStringPreference

- Show "Save as default" button inside column-selector panel when there
  are unsaved column changes (lei-records, countries)

Sprint 32 Task 6: Table/VirtualTable sort+fill (16 new tests, 1700 total)

- TableColumn<'a> field order: Header, Width, SortKey, Fill, Render
  (Render last to avoid semicolon-in-fun parsing ambiguity in single-line literals)
- SortDirection DU (Ascending | Descending)
- SortState record { Column: int; Direction: SortDirection }
- TableColumn module: create, withSort, asFill helpers
- VirtualTable.toggleSort: pure (colIdx, SortState option -> SortState option)
- VirtualTable.sortItems: pure (columns, SortState option, items -> items)
- VirtualTable.view: shows ▲▼ sort indicators, respects Fill columns
- Table.view: uses El.fill for Fill columns
- 16 TDD tests: TableColumn helpers, toggleSort cycling, sortItems correctness,
  sort indicator rendering, fill column wrapping

fix: update bayyinah-server/src/main/java/com/ks/bayyinah/bayyinah_server/dto/UserUpdateRequest.java

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

TESTER: 7.1 — 33 tests for zero-arg UX (CLI behavior, status bar repo, padding overflow, watch fallback, README)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

release: v2.16.0 — security hardening, thread safety, 107 tests

Comprehensive code review and fix of 42 issues across the entire codebase.

Security (5):
- SSRF prevention: flow_id UUID validation in HA auth
- SSE connection limit (max 4, 30-min timeout)
- Volume clamping 0-100 in all entry points
- MAC address regex validation before bluetoothctl
- /api/status removed from public paths; /api/health added

Thread safety (6):
- _clients snapshot under lock in ~15 endpoints
- stop_sendspin() SSE notification via _update_status()
- Zombie counter race moved inside _status_lock
- Config file read under config_lock
- MA API credentials mutex
- BT executor pool 2→4 threads

Error handling (7):
- get_json() fallback, str(e) leak removal (15 places)
- IPC int() try/except, client_id path sanitization
- player_names type validation, log-level allowlist
- force=True removed from set_password

Robustness (10):
- fsync before config rename, fire-and-forget task refs
- postMessage origin check, escHtml quote encoding
- D-Bus monitor exit flag, 0-as-falsy fix
- Event loop cleanup, rate-limit dict cap, AUDIO_UID env

Infrastructure (6):
- SYS_ADMIN capability removed, scripts/ excluded from mypy
- Dependency bounds tightened
- dev-requirements.txt added

Tests (8):
- 42→107 tests (65 new)
- conftest.py + 6 new test files
- datetime.UTC→timezone.utc for Python 3.9 compat

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: redesign README for UX clarity

Restructured for scanability and pilot-first experience:

- Hero: one-line tagline + 1-sentence description
- Problem: table format showing 3 bad options and their catch
- Solution: blockquote with the core loop (Plug in → LED → Fly again)
- Visual 4-step ASCII flow diagram for the pilot's workflow
- Getting Started: 4 clear numbered steps with headings
- LED guide: emoji indicators for each pattern
- Downloading logs: separate focused section with web UI wireframe
- Troubleshooting: collapsed <details> sections to reduce visual noise
- Developer guide: entire section collapsed behind <details>
- Compatibility, config, and log storage kept but de-prioritised

commit.message:
feat: prompt AI to request WhatsApp location share when user asks about arrivals (#6)

When users ask about bus arrivals without providing a location, the AI
had no instruction to ask for one or guide them to share it.

## Changes

- **`internal/chat/ai.go` — `getSystemPrompt()`**: Replaced the passive
"when users share their location" line with an explicit instruction to
detect location-less arrival queries and guide the user through
WhatsApp's native location sharing:
> Tap the attachment (paperclip) icon → select "Location" → "Send Your
Current Location"

```go
// Before
When users share their location, use it to find nearby buses.

// After
When users ask about bus arrivals, nearby buses, or real-time bus information without providing a location,
ask them to share their location using WhatsApp's built-in location feature.
Instruct them: tap the attachment (paperclip) icon in the chat, select "Location", then choose
"Send Your Current Location". Once they share their location, use it to find nearby buses.
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

🔒 GitHub Advanced Security automatically protects Copilot coding agent
pull requests. You can protect all pull requests by enabling Advanced
Security for your repositories. [Learn more about Advanced
Security.](https://gh.io/cca-advanced-security)

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sirily11 <32106111+sirily11@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 15 outside-in tests validating runner module split (#16)

* test: add 15 outside-in tests validating runner module split

Validates all 3 extracted modules (listeners, json_parser, audit) from
the user's perspective via the compiled binary:

Listeners (5 tests):
- --progress flag shows step start marker on stderr
- Completion icon (✓) on success
- Failure icon (✗) on error
- Skipped icon (⊘) on false condition
- Progress output goes to stderr, not stdout

JSON parser (3 tests):
- Fenced  without json label)

Audit (7 tests):
- Filename format: {recipe}-{timestamp}.jsonl
- Entry has all fields: step_id, status, duration_ms, error, output_len
- step_id matches executed step
- error is null on success
- error populated on failure
- Multiple steps produce multiple JSONL lines
- duration_ms is a number

Total outside-in tests: 51 (up from 36)
Total tests: 399

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: persistent key store, key regeneration, and Azure vs AWS discussion

- Add POST /agents/{id}/regenerate-key admin endpoint for key recovery
- Add regenerate_agent_key MCP tool
- Create agents/key_store.py — persists agent keys to local JSON file
- Create agents/mcp_helpers.py — register-or-recover pattern via MCP

commit.message:
fix: OOM crash in Test262 and fuzz build failure (#454)

* fix: add allocation guards to string operations to prevent OOM

- Make MAX_STRING_LEN (1 << 28, ~256 MiB) a public module-level constant
  in builtins::string for reuse across all string creation paths.

- Fix String.prototype.repeat inline handler in interpreter/mod.rs:
  delegate to string_repeat() which validates bounds, handle negative
  and infinite HeapNumber values with RangeError.

- Fix String.prototype.padStart/padEnd inline handlers in
  interpreter/mod.rs: delegate to string_pad_start()/string_pad_end()
  which now validate target_length against MAX_STRING_LEN.

- Change string_pad_start() and string_pad_end() return types from
  String to StatorResult<String> with MAX_STRING_LEN guard.

- Add safe f64-to-usize conversion in install_globals.rs padStart/padEnd
  handlers to prevent wrapping on NaN/negative values.

- Add tests for repeat/padStart/padEnd exceeding MAX_STRING_LEN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: OOM crash in Test262 and fuzz build failure

- Fix fuzz targets for Rc<str> migration (fuzz_property_ops.rs, fuzz_builtins_array.rs)
- Add MAX_STRING_LEN guard to js_add() string concatenation
- String.prototype.repeat delegates to safe string_repeat() with bounds
- String.prototype.padStart/padEnd delegate to safe pad functions with bounds
- Prevents memory allocation of 7.8+ petabytes from malicious/pathological inputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add language selector to general settings and setup wizard; localize all wizard strings (#427)

* Initial plan

* feat: add language selector to general settings and setup wizard, localize all wizard strings

- Add LanguageSelector to GeneralSettings card with language label/description
- Add LanguageSelector to setup wizard header (page 1)
- Localize all hardcoded strings in setup-wizard.tsx using wizard.* translation keys
- Localize all hardcoded strings in step-board-setup.tsx using wizard.boardSetup.* keys
- Localize all hardcoded strings in step-easy-plugins.tsx using wizard.easyPlugins.* keys
- Localize all hardcoded strings in step-welcome.tsx using wizard.welcome.* keys
- Fix 2 hardcoded strings in general-settings.tsx (End Time, When silence begins)
- Add languageLabel, languageDescription, and error fallback translation keys to all 14 locales
- Update test mocks to support LanguageSelector's useRouter dependency

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: stabilize silence toggle test by waiting for full data initialization

The 'shows silence time pickers when enabled' test was flaky in CI because
it only waited for switches to appear before clicking the silence toggle.
The component uses useDeferredValue for silence config, which can cause the
initialization effect to fire after the switch is already visible. In slower
CI environments, clicking the toggle during this window causes the deferred
effect to reset silenceEnabled, hiding the time pickers.

Fixed by waiting for the polling input to have its API-loaded value (300)
before interacting, which confirms all deferred values have settled. Also
added a longer waitFor timeout for the assertion.

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey D Johnson <jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

Set agent-side buildTimeout for Blazor Wasm to fix build timeout (#2162)

PR #2158 increased the controller-level session timeout to 20 minutes,
but the crank agent's build step still used the default 10-minute
DefaultBuildTimeout from dotnet/crank. The 'Build is taking too long'
error on asp-citrine-lin (build 2921804) was the agent killing the
build, not the controller timing out.

Add --application.buildTimeout 00:20:00 to the crank args so the agent
allows 20 minutes for the Blazor WASM application to build.

Ref: https://github.com/aspnet/Benchmarks/pull/2158

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sanitize dots in repo name for federated identity credentials (#7040)

azd pipeline config fails when the GitHub repo name contains dots
because federated identity credential names only allow letters,
numbers, hyphens, and underscores. The repo slug was only replacing
'/' with '-' but not handling dots or other special characters.

Use a shared regex sanitizer (same pattern already used for branch
names) to replace all invalid characters in the credential name.
Also removes unnecessary url.PathEscape wrapping.

Fixes #5948

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Additional msal-react v5 migration details (#8386)

This pull request updates the migration documentation for both MSAL
Browser and MSAL React to clarify the required steps for upgrading to
v5, including payload type changes, migration paths, and compatibility
notes. The changes provide explicit migration examples and highlight new
requirements and breaking changes.

**MSAL React v5 Migration Guidance and Compatibility:**

- Added clear migration paths for upgrading from v1/v2/v3 to v5, with
emphasis on following the MSAL Browser v4-v5 migration guide and setting
up the required COOP redirect bridge.
- Documented React version support: MSAL React v5 now requires React
19+, with notes and workarounds for temporary use on React 18, though it
is not supported.

**MSAL Browser v5 Event Handling Changes:**

- Provided explicit guidance on handling the new `LOGIN_SUCCESS` payload
type, including before/after TypeScript code samples to illustrate the
migration from casting to `AuthenticationResult` to using `AccountInfo`.

**InteractionStatus and Event Consolidation:**

- Clarified the consolidation of `InteractionStatus` values and provided
a migration example for simplifying in-progress status checks in React
apps.

These updates ensure developers have clear, actionable steps for
migrating to the latest major versions and handling breaking changes in
event payloads and interaction status.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tnorling <5307810+tnorling@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: critical bugs in run_ablation.sh and analyze_all.py

B1 run_ablation.sh: PBS path resolution - use PBS_O_WORKDIR as primary
     SCRIPT_DIR was computed from $0 (relative path), but PBS batch jobs
     start in HOME dir, making cd fail and SCRIPT_DIR=''. All paths
     become invalid and no runs execute.
     Fix: SCRIPT_DIR="${PBS_O_WORKDIR:-$(cd ...)}"

B2/B3 run_ablation.sh: TSV header mismatch
     Header was 'impl\tgraph\tn_nodes\tn_edges\ttime_sec\tgteps\tbfs_sec\tback_sec'
     (8 cols, lowercase 'impl'). load_tsv_skip_duplicate_headers() only
     recognizes 'Implementation' as header marker, so entire ablation_df
     would be empty. main.cpp outputs only 4 cols, so all rows would
     also fail the len(parts) < len(header) check.
     Fix: printf 'Implementation\tGraph\tTime_sec\tGTEPS\n'
     Fix: awk column ref $6 -> $4 for GTEPS

B5 run_ablation.sh: stderr mixed into TSV (2>&1 -> separate log)
     Redirected stderr to ablation_phase_timing.log instead of SUMMARY

B4 analyze_all.py: GPU_ReadMostly missing from KNOWN_IMPLS
     Without this, load_tsv_skip_duplicate_headers() silently skips all
     GPU_ReadMostly rows, making ablation analysis produce empty output.
     Fix: Added 'GPU_ReadMostly' and 'gpu_readmostly' to KNOWN_IMPLS

B6 run_profile.sh: ncu loop added gpu_readmostly for complete ablation profile

NOTE: Job #1423835 (run_ablation.sh) was submitted with the buggy script.
      qdel 1423835 && qsub scripts/run_ablation.sh  <- required

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate silent ImportError fallbacks across codebase (#2982)

* fix: eliminate silent ImportError fallbacks in hooks (Phase 1)

Replace silent ImportError handlers across 23 hook files with explicit
warnings and fail-loud patterns:

- Convert relative-to-absolute import fallbacks to direct imports with
  sys.path setup for dual-mode (package/standalone) compatibility
- Replace 'except ImportError: pass' with stderr warnings
- Add WARNING prints to all feature-flag ImportError handlers
- Convert platform-specific fcntl import to explicit sys.platform check
- Convert git_utils nested fallbacks to log-and-raise pattern
- Preserve already-correct handlers (sys.exit(1), raise HookImportError)

No silent ImportError swallowing remains in production hook code.

Fixes #2982 (Phase 1: hooks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: eliminate silent ImportError fallbacks in amplifier-bundle (Phase 2)

Add explicit stderr warnings to all silent ImportError handlers across
33 amplifier-bundle files:

- Hook modules: All 8 module packages now warn when delegation fails
- Tools: builders, reflection, context preservation, XPIA defense all
  log warnings instead of silently degrading
- Skills: claude-agent-sdk, pm-architect scripts already had proper
  handlers; verified and left intact
- Replace 'except ImportError: pass' with warning prints
- Add stderr warnings alongside existing logger.warning() calls

No silent ImportError swallowing remains in amplifier-bundle.

Fixes #2982 (Phase 2: amplifier-bundle)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: eliminate silent ImportError fallbacks in src/amplihack (Phase 3)

Add explicit stderr warnings to all silent ImportError handlers across
37 src/amplihack files:

- agents/goal_seeking: SDK adapters, hive_mind, cognitive adapters
  now warn when optional AI/ML backends are unavailable
- proxy: github_auth, litellm, log_streaming warn on missing deps
- launcher: auto_mode, fork_manager, memory_config warn on missing deps
- memory: kuzu, cli_visualize, auto_backend warn on missing backends
- workflows, eval, security: all warn instead of silently degrading
- vendor/blarify: warns on optional multilspy deps

Every except ImportError now either raises, logs to stderr, or both.
No silent degradation remains.

Fixes #2982 (Phase 3: src/amplihack)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devy.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>

Sprint 32: bracketed paste, mouse drag, Channel<int> input

- PasteSub: new Sub<'msg> case for bracketed paste (?2004h)
  - Tea.fs: PasteSub added to Sub DU; Sub.map case added
  - Detect.fs: parse thread handles [200~ / [201~ to accumulate paste text
  - App.fs: PasteSub dispatches Pasted events to handlers
  - 3 new tests (dispatch, no-fire-for-keys, Sub.map transform)

- DragSub: motion event coalescing in App.fs event drain loop
  - Multiple consecutive Motion events for same button → only last dispatched
  - Different-button Motion events preserved independently
  - ?1002h button-event tracking enabled in EnterRawMode
  - 4 new tests (fires for motion, not for press, coalescing, multi-button)

- Channel<int> replaces ConcurrentQueue + Thread.Sleep spin-wait
  - BoundedChannel(4096) with blocking ReadAsync eliminates 1ms jitter
  - tryReadChar: -1 → blocking, 0 → TryRead poll, N → CancellationToken timeout
  - No behavioral change visible in tests; zero-jitter for modifier key sequences

- ?2004h/?1002h enabled, corresponding ?2004l/?1002l on leave
- Total: 1,684 tests, all passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix color overrides: palette slots follow theme, accents stay fixed

Palette swatch picks now store slot indices (numbers) that resolve
to the active palette's colors  so switching themes updates those
zones. Accent color picks store hex strings that persist across
palette changes. Backward compatible: legacy hex overrides from
saved compositions are treated as accent (fixed) colors.

commit.message:
py-uproot: Add patch for uproot RNTuple `_from_zigzag` unsigned integer fix (PR #1593) (#854)

RNTuple zigzag decoding incorrectly handled signed integers in the upper
half of their range (e.g. int32 values ≥ 2³⁰), producing wrong negative
results because NumPy's right-shift on signed arrays is arithmetic
(sign-extending), not logical.

## Changes

- **New patch** in `py_uproot/package.py` for
[`scikit-hep/uproot5#1593`](https://github.com/scikit-hep/uproot5/pull/1593):
fixes `_from_zigzag` in `src/uproot/models/RNTuple.py` to view the
buffer as the unsigned counterpart before the shift/XOR, then
reinterpret as the original signed dtype

```python
# Before (buggy — arithmetic right-shift on signed array)
return n >> 1 ^ -(n & 1)

# After (correct — logical right-shift via unsigned view)
u = n.view(numpy.dtype(f"uint{n.dtype.itemsize * 8}"))
return ((u >> 1) ^ -(u & 1)).view(n.dtype)
```

## Version range: `@5.5.0:5.7.1`

- **Lower bound `5.5.0`**: `_from_zigzag` does not exist in v5.4.5;
first appears in v5.5.0
- **Upper bound `5.7.1`**: latest release before the fix merged (Mar 9
2026)
- Patch applies cleanly at both bounds (confirmed via `patch --dry-run`
with offsets −22 and −4 lines respectively)

commit.message:
fix(web): bridge hover gap in navbar product dropdown

The mt-1 gap between the Product button and dropdown caused
onMouseLeave to fire when the cursor crossed the gap. Wrapped
the dropdown in an outer div with pt-1 invisible padding so
the hover zone is continuous.
